quoted_tweet.text:
事前登録【450名】突破しました🎉
ありがとうございます！

6月初旬にリリース予定の教材です。

未経験から、ゲームを「作る側」へ。

AIを使ってRobloxゲームを作り、
公開までやり切るための内容になっています。

わたし自身も、
・プログラミングできない
・イラスト描けない
・3D未経験

という状態からスタートしました。

この教材では、
その制作フローをそのまま再現できる形にしています。

さらにメルマガでは、
AIでRobloxゲームを作るようになるまでの
「クリエイターとしての歩み」をストーリー形式で配信中です。

・どんな試行錯誤があったのか
・どうやって壁を越えたのか
・何ができるようになっていったのか

こうしたリアルな変化の過程を追えるので、
未経験から作る側に進んでいく流れが見えてきます。

✅未経験OK
✅コードは書かない（AIに任せる）
✅AIに指示しながら進める
✅14日で公開までのルートを再現

「知識だけ」で終わらせず、
ちゃんと1本出すところまでやります。

今のうちに登録しておくと

・リリース時【10％オフ】
・限定特典あり

で受け取れます👇
https://t.co/nrkUdOsaC7

quoted_tweet.text:
未経験でも、AIだけでゲームを作って公開できる時代になりました。

Robloxゲーム制作を14日でやり切る教材について、価格と特典を公開します。

14日後には、自分のゲームを実際に公開できるところまで持っていける内容です。

未経験の方でも迷わず進められるように、かなり丁寧に作り込んでいます。

気づいたら、10万字を超えるボリュームになっていました。

価格は、リリース特価9,800円です。
メルマガに先行登録していただいた方には、10%オフのリンクをお渡しします。
発売は、6月初旬の予定です。

気になる方は、先行登録しておいてください。
リリース時に割引リンクをお送りします。

https://t.co/eHFW93Ndv8

kept.text:
Roblox shares fell sharply today after the company slashed its full-year guidance.

The stock dropped about 18% in one day.

Roblox cut its full year bookings forecast by around 1 billion dollars. 
Bookings show how much future money the company expects from game currency sales.

New safety rules for children are slowing things down and company says the changes are needed after lawsuits and pressure to protect kids from online risks. 

Investors are worried about slower growth and sold shares fast.

kept.text:
YouTube removed a 760k Roblox channel @TeamYouTube 

This is about the Bloomation channel. Roblox videos where all animation was created manually, with hundreds of hours of work invested. No reuploads, no scams or fraud.

The reason given was “spam, fraud, and deception policies.” How do original Roblox animations fall under this reason?

This is the creator’s only channel.
Result: the channel was completely removed from @YouTube.

It’s been almost a month since the deletion. The appeal was rejected, and the decision remains unchanged.

The situation becomes even more concerning when you realize this is not an isolated case. Many animators are now facing the same issue: original channels getting demonetized, removed, or penalized by YouTube support and automated AI systems.

These are not scammers. These are creative people who spent years building their channels, only to lose them.

I strongly ask @TeamYouTube to take this situation seriously and reconsider the decisions made by the AI system, which is increasingly making critical mistakes.

I ask everyone who reads this post to help bring attention to this problem. This can affect any creator at any moment. Many animation channels are already facing this, and YouTube needs to pay attention to what is happening.

Affected creators:
@CCountryz18217 @stategirlsyou @BoomLab172561 @SOKALUPEC @ifCurse @ULTRA_GAMERS_ @nick_film76462 @KeidBoi @SacredBricks @LevelLabs2gvb @spectrlabs1011 @EternalMystYT @PBACONMAN3 @mansterzs @TheShadowWork @RottenAges @DeadNetStudios @RayDocsProd

kept.text:
built this fortnite map 1 year ago. poured 1 month into it. custom models. unique mechanics. made $300 total.

here's the mistake.

i was building what i liked. what kids want is completely different. those two worlds almost never overlap.

look at roblox. brainrot games look like absolute trash. ugly graphics. no polish. one core loop. they hit 100k ccu and print money every day. kids don't cares how it looks. they care that the loop hits.

i had it backwards. i thought more features = more playtime. wrong. one good feature can pull 1 hour average session length. 15 mechanics just adds complexity. the kid gets confused and leave.

stop trying to make your map look perfect. stop polishing it forever. stop adding 100 new mechanics. none of that is what kids click for. none of it is what keeps them playing.

build the loop first. ignore everything else until that prints. the kid who wants to feel strong fast. the kid who wants to flex on his friends. the kid who wants a quick dopamine hit before homework. they don't care about custom models. they care about feeling something specific, fast. that's the whole game.

every map i've built since has been for the kid. now i manage millions in maps and work with celebrities.

failure is part of the path.
